faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Does converting PNG to AVIF preserve transparency?
faq.a1: Yes. AVIF fully supports the alpha channel. All transparent areas in your PNG — logos, icons, UI elements — will be perfectly preserved in the AVIF output.
faq.q2: How much smaller will AVIF files be compared to PNG?
faq.a2: AVIF files are typically 50–80% smaller than the equivalent PNG. A 2 MB PNG could become 400–800 KB in AVIF with barely noticeable quality difference.
faq.q3: Is it safe to convert private images here?
faq.a3: Yes. Avifkit converts files entirely inside your browser — your images are never uploaded to any server. Nothing leaves your device.
faq.q4: Can I use AVIF as a drop-in PNG replacement on my website?
faq.a4: Yes, for modern browsers. Use the HTML <picture> element to serve AVIF with a PNG fallback. Most visitors will automatically receive the smaller AVIF file.
faq.a5: AVIF (AV1 Image File Format) is a next-generation image format offering superior compression compared to PNG, JPG, and WebP. It supports transparency, HDR, and wide color gamut.
faq.q6: What is the maximum file size I can convert?
faq.a6: Each file can be up to 10 MB. Since conversion happens entirely in your browser, larger files may take slightly longer depending on your device.
faq.q7: Can I batch convert multiple PNG files to AVIF?
faq.a7: Yes. Upload up to 30 PNG files at once, convert them all in one click, then download individually or as a ZIP archive.
faq.q8: What quality setting should I use for PNG to AVIF?
faq.a8: For transparent graphics and icons, 70–85% quality gives excellent results. For photographic PNG content, 60–75% offers strong compression with minimal visible difference.
faq.q9: Do I need to create an account to use Avifkit?
faq.a9: No. Avifkit is completely free with no registration, no watermarks, and no usage limits.
